---
title: "Designing OERs for Reuse"
format: html
---

To enable meaningful reuse:

### Use editable, non-proprietary formats

Prefer:

{{< meta glossary.docx >}}, {{< meta glossary.md >}}, {{< meta glossary.qmd >}}, {{< meta glossary.html >}}.

Avoid:

locked PDFs or proprietary formats without export options.

### Include source files

Provide:

-   raw images;
-   scripts;
-   datasets; and/or
-   slide decks in editable formats.

### Ensure accessibility

Best practice includes:

-   alternative "Alt" text;
-   clear headings;
-   colour-contrast compliance;
-   readable fonts and spacing.

There are some useful tips in the Una Europa’s Creating OERS how to guide in the section 1A. Make your resource as accessible as possible at https://www.una-europa.eu/knowledge-hub/toolkits/oer/creating-oers-how-to-guide [@RN68108].

### Avoid local hard-coding

Where possible, facilitate future users to swap out:

-   institutional names;
-   roles;
-   signposting/web links;
-   context specific examples and case studies.

[![](images/clipboard-2765196364.png){fig-align="center" width="50"}](/OERs%20for%20creators/Creator-Copyright.html)
